messages.content:
CVE-2024-57887 is a high-severity vulnerability in the Linux kernel related to a use-after-free issue found in the function adv7533_attach_dsi. This occurs when the host_node pointer is assigned and freed improperly in adv7533_parse_dt() and then reused, potentially leading to program crashes, execution of unexpected values, or code execution.

The type of vulnerability is classified under CWE-416, which involves referencing memory after it has been freed. Such vulnerabilities can permit an attacker to execute arbitrary code or cause denial-of-service conditions. The associated MITRE ATT&CK Technique is T1068, which relates to exploitation for privilege escalation.

Affected software includes multiple versions of the Linux kernel, specifically versions 4.8 through 6.1.125, 6.2 through 6.6.70, and various release candidates of version 6.13 up to rc5. Users operating any of these kernel versions should consider upgrading to patched versions or implementing mitigation strategies.

Key mitigation techniques include implementing security measures such as M1051 (Application Isolation and Sandboxing), M1050 (Execution Prevention), M1048 (Behavior Prevention on Endpoint), M1019 (Threat Intelligence Program), and M1038 (Network Segmentation) to reduce the risk and impact of potential exploitation.
